core_mechanism: a probabilistic-method existence proof using correlated random high-dimensional projections/subspaces to exhibit a failure of additivity of an extremal (min/max-optimized) functional under a product/tensor combination — i.e. constructing random objects whose joint value strictly beats the sum of the parts
brief_reason: The abstract proves additivity of the minimum-output p-Rényi entropy fails by combining two correlated random-projection constructions (product-conjugate and transpose-complement witnesses), i.e. a random-subspace existence argument for a super-additivity violation of an optimized functional under tensor products.
